scope_boundary: Readiness lifecycle probe only. The probe validates six deterministic synthetic readiness lifecycle transitions and rejects twelve expected-invalid controls for non-forward jumps, missing fresh gate evidence, missing residual escrow, default readiness without regression or authority records, quarantine ordinary routing or missing fallback, missing supersession records, missing retirement receipts, retired-state transition, missing non-claim boundary, and support-state promotion. It does not execute a deployed readiness engine, residual-ledger storage, live quarantine routing, terminal-state governance, rollback executor, runtime monitor, MoECOT replay, benchmark, or current-readiness assessment.
transition_reason: The readiness lifecycle probe is useful because it makes candidate, shadow, canary, qualified, default-ready, quarantined, superseded, and retired routes executable as finite records with fresh evidence, residual escrow, fallback, expiry, regression, authority, route, supersession, retirement, support-state, and non-claim boundaries. It remains a deterministic synthetic record gate, not deployed readiness-engine evidence, so this accepted decision blocks promotion for readiness-engine behavior, lifecycle transition execution, residual-ledger storage, live quarantine routing, gate-quality checking, terminal-state governance, rollback execution, runtime monitoring, MoECOT replay, benchmark performance, current module readiness, or the Readiness Gates chapter core claim.
required_artifacts: deployed or externally reviewable readiness lifecycle execution trace
gate-quality checks tied to concrete route decisions
terminal-state governance trace for supersession and retirement
rollback executor or runtime monitor evidence
MoECOT replay packet or externally reviewable readiness packet
benchmark evidence only after readiness semantics are tied to measured tasks
accepted evidence-transition record for any narrowed upward support movement
negative_results: missing fresh gate evidence is rejected
default readiness without regression floor is rejected
default readiness without authority scope is rejected
quarantine without diagnostic fallback is rejected
transition from retired state is rejected
support-state promotion from readiness lifecycle shape is rejected
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: non-forward jump, missing fresh evidence, missing residual escrow, unsafe default readiness, quarantine leakage, missing terminal record, retired-state transition, missing non-claim, or support-promotion control accepted as valid

probe described as deployed readiness-engine, lifecycle-execution, residual-ledger-storage, live-quarantine-routing, gate-quality, terminal-governance, rollback-execution, runtime-monitoring, MoECOT-replay, benchmark, current-readiness, or support-state evidence
support-state promotion without a separate accepted upward transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed readiness-lifecycle claim after deployed or externally reviewable lifecycle execution traces, residual-ledger storage/replay, live quarantine routing, gate-quality evidence, terminal-state governance traces, rollback or runtime-monitor behavior, MoECOT replay or equivalent external readiness packets, residual accounting, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted upward transition.
limitations: does not promote any chapter core claim
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent readiness-engine, residual-ledger, router, benchmark, MoECOT, runtime-monitor, source-interpretation, or external safety audit
acceptance_blockers: no deployed or externally reviewable readiness lifecycle execution trace
no residual-ledger storage or replay evidence
no rollback executor or runtime monitor evidence
non_claims: does not promote readiness lifecycle evidence above argument

does not prove deployed readiness-engine behavior, lifecycle transition execution, residual-ledger storage, live quarantine routing, gate-quality checks, terminal-state governance, rollback execution, runtime monitoring, MoECOT replay, benchmark performance, current module readiness, model quality, safety, or ASI
does not create an upward support-state transition
does not promote any chapter core claim
does not promote the Readiness Gates chapter core claim
